item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents open DISAGREE with the statement, so there is no genuine initial opposition on the WVS item. The debate pivots to whether Indonesian collectivism sacrifices individual rights rather than the normative question itself. A's T3 defends Indonesian collectivism as protecting both group and individual ('Pemikiran kolektif tidak selalu mengorbankan hak individu'). A's T5 then concedes B's T4 counterargument — 'Tidak setuju dengan klaim bahwa koleksivisme Indonesia tidak mengorbankan hak individu' (= A now agrees collectivism does sacrifice rights), reversing the specific T3 claim. This is the most notable intra-debate shift but is not a flip on the main statement. Agent B became more firmly anti-statement over the debate (P dropped 0.4408→0.3522) while A moved very slightly upward (0.451→0.4779); agents diverged rather than converged. No language violations — all Latin alphabet throughout. B's T2 references 'The Indonesian perspective' without endorsing it, which is borderline but not a prohibited endorsement-opener.
